sections.content:
World model: I was wrong about the center structure. The center is a 3x3 sub-grid of 2x2 sub-cells with different colors. Bottom-left center is b b b / B B B / W g g. Bottom-right center is R R c / c c c / g g c. Top centers are R R R / B B B / g W g. The center cells might be clickable!
Plan: Try clicking on center sub-cells to see if they change.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on center sub-cell (0,0) at row 40, col 46
predict(expect_change=True, note='Test if center sub-cell (0,0) is clickable')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 40, 'col': 46}])

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("After clicking center (0,0):")
print("Bottom-right center:")
for r in range(40, 46):
    print(f"  {r}: '{lines[r][46:52]}'")
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
